reasoning.typography: Clean sans-serif system with good weight hierarchy, but lacks distinctive character — feels like refined Inter/SF Pro, not a unique voice
reasoning.hierarchy: Strong single-focus hero with commanding headline, clear visual dominance, CTAs well-separated with intentional sizing
reasoning.distinctiveness: Generic productivity launcher messaging — 'shortcut to everything' could be Alfred, Spotlight, or any launcher; no unique POV visible
reasoning.color: Disciplined dark theme with restrained accent use, clean monochrome dominance, minimal color pollution
reasoning.density: Confident breathing room in hero, good vertical rhythm, avoids claustrophobic SaaS padding but plays it safe
reasoning.voice: Standard product site structure apparent — platform nav, hero, CTAs, features — IA doesn't reflect an opinionated story beyond product delivery

winning_patterns: Hero headline has real visual weight and presence
Dark UI execution is refined and confident, not trendy
Download CTAs show platform specificity (Mac/Windows) vs generic 'Get Started'
Version info and technical details visible (homebrew install) — respects developer audience
Subhead actually describes the product succinctly
anti_patterns: Headline could be any productivity tool — zero product specificity
Navigation bar is standard SaaS horizontal menu (Store/Pro/AI/iOS/Windows/Teams/etc)
Section headline 'Take shortcuts, not detours' is clever copywriting but still generic launcher speak
'Introducing Glaze' pill feels like obligatory announcement banner
Overall structure follows productivity-app stencil religiously
